title: Make Go not panic on a partial update
body:
Right now, if you call Update with only part of the options struct added, it panics. This fixes that by only adding them if they are not nil.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body: Update seems to have no tests in all cases, what should be done here? I'm not aware of all Update behaviours if a full test suite needs to be written.
Thanks for the fix.  Please squash your commits into one and use your real name to sign it off.

> Thanks, the change itself seems fine but could please add a basic update test in e.g. pkg/bindings/test/containers_test.go to ensure it works when passing nil options?
> 
> Also please squash your commits.

Yep sure, so my question here is it seems Update had no tests before, so what should I do regarding that? I am aware how the resources side works, so not the rest, so I will need guidance if you want Update tested rather than just this specific part of Update.

@Luap99

> Update had no tests before, so what should I do regarding that? I am aware how the resources side works, so not the rest, so I will need guidance if you want Update tested rather than just this specific part of Update.

I think just adding an empty (i.e. only default option struct) Update() call would be good enough to ensure it doesn't panic or errors out. I don't want to force you to write a bunch of specific test cases in the bindings for that.

> > Update had no tests before, so what should I do regarding that? I am aware how the resources side works, so not the rest, so I will need guidance if you want Update tested rather than just this specific part of Update.
> 
> I think just adding an empty (i.e. only default option struct) Update() call would be good enough to ensure it doesn't panic or errors out. I don't want to force you to write a bunch of specific test cases in the bindings for that.

Okay, shall do!
Realised I forgot to run sign off, oops, done now

This failure seems unrelated to me since it doesn't touch code I wrote or functions I edited

yes looks like quay.io has problems once again, I will rerun them.
